_meta.description: Mapping of ARCANA agent lenses to HUMMBL bibliography entries. Each lens lists primary (most directly grounding) and secondary (adjacent/supporting) citations. Only keys that exist in the bibliography are listed.
_meta.coverage_note: The bibliography is optimized for AI governance and engineering (T4, T5, T7, T11). ARCANA lenses requiring deep social theory (Weber, Foucault, Habermas, Bourdieu, Marx) are now grounded via Wave 4A expansion (15 entries added).

ashby.lens: Cybernetician — Requisite Variety, homeostasis, viable systems
ashby.gaps: No Ashby 'Design for a Brain' entry. Beer's VSM is covered.

bateson.lens: Ecologist of Mind — double bind, levels of learning, cybernetics
bateson.gaps: Bateson's 'Steps to an Ecology of Mind' is now in bibliography (T2). Strong coverage.

bki.lens: Belonging as Knowledge Infrastructure — neurobiological prerequisites for cognition
bki.gaps: No Walton+Cohen belonging uncertainty entry. No Bourdieu habitus entry. BKI Proposition 1 (neurobiological) weakly supported — add Cozolino or Lieberman on social neuroscience.

bostrom.lens: Futurist of Existential Risk — superintelligence, control problem, simulation
bostrom.gaps: No simulation argument or existential risk survey entry beyond Bostrom's own book.

bourdieu.lens: Field Theorist — habitus, cultural capital, symbolic violence
bourdieu.gaps: Bourdieu's 'Outline of a Theory of Practice' (1977) now in bibliography (T2). Consider adding 'Distinction' (1984) for cultural capital analysis.

burnham.lens: Managerial Revolutionary — separation of ownership and control, technocracy
burnham.gaps: Burnham's 'The Managerial Revolution' (1941) now in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage.

chomsky.lens: Propaganda Analyst — manufacturing consent, political economy of media
chomsky.gaps: Chomsky & Herman's 'Manufacturing Consent' (1988) now in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage.

derrida.gaps: No Derrida entries. Low coverage in continental philosophy overall.

foucault.lens: Archaeologist of Power — power/knowledge, discourse, biopolitics
foucault.gaps: No direct Foucault entry. Raji/Mitchell/Gebru represent his power-analysis in the AI domain. Add 'Discipline and Punish' or 'Power/Knowledge' to T2.

gramsci.lens: Left Mirror — hegemony, organic intellectuals, war of position
gramsci.gaps: Gramsci's 'Prison Notebooks' now in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage.

habermas.lens: Communicative Rationalist — public sphere, discourse ethics, communicative action
habermas.gaps: Habermas's 'Theory of Communicative Action' now in bibliography (T2). Floridi's AI4People framework is Habermasian in structure. Strong coverage.

heidegger.gaps: Heidegger's 'The Question Concerning Technology' (1954) and 'Being and Time' (1927) now in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage for AI governance framing.

ibn_khaldun.lens: Civilizational Historian — asabiyyah, Khaldun Cycle, economic foundations of power
ibn_khaldun.gaps: Ibn Khaldun's 'Muqaddimah' now in bibliography (T1). Unique non-Western voice — strong coverage.

kissinger.lens: Grand Strategist — balance of power, realpolitik, international order
kissinger.gaps: No Kissinger entry. 'World Order' (2014) or 'The Age of AI' (2021) relevant for geopolitics of AI.

land.lens: Accelerationist — techno-capital acceleration, hyperstition, Dark Enlightenment
land.gaps: No Land entry. Intentionally marginal — represent via adjacent complexity/emergence literature.

luhmann.lens: Systems Theorist — autopoiesis, functional differentiation, social systems
luhmann.gaps: Zettelkasten entry covers cognition angle. No 'Social Systems' (1984) or 'Theory of Society' entry. Add to T12 or T2. Park2023 adds autopoietic agent simulation.

marx.lens: Historical Materialist — base/superstructure, alienation, class struggle
marx.gaps: Marx's 'Capital Vol. 1' now in bibliography (T1). Relevant for AI labor displacement framing.

mcluhan.lens: Medium Theorist — the medium is the message, hot/cool media, extensions of man
mcluhan.gaps: McLuhan's 'Understanding Media' now in bibliography (T1). Essential for AI-as-medium analysis.

measurement.lens: Metrologist — Goodhart's Law, Campbell's Law, proxy metrics
measurement.gaps: No direct Goodhart/Campbell entry. HELM and AgentBench are concrete implementations of measurement theory applied to LLMs.

nietzsche.lens: Genealogist — will to power, perspectivism, ressentiment, nihilism
nietzsche.gaps: Nietzsche's 'On the Genealogy of Morals' now in bibliography (T1). Source text for IN inversion methodology.

pareto.lens: Elite Theorist — circulation of elites, Lions vs Foxes, 80/20
pareto.gaps: Pareto's 'The Mind and Society' now in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage.

popper.lens: Falsificationist — critical rationalism, falsification, open society
popper.gaps: Popper's 'The Logic of Scientific Discovery' now in bibliography (T1). Foundational for IN falsification epistemology.

schmitt.lens: Jurist of the Exception — sovereignty, friend/enemy, state of exception
schmitt.gaps: No Schmitt entry. Kill-switch and governance authority patterns are Schmittian in structure.

strauss.lens: Careful Reader — esoteric writing, persecution, natural right vs historicism
strauss.gaps: No Strauss entry. Represent via Kuhn (paradigm concealment) and close-reading methodology.

taleb.lens: Uncertainty Theorist — antifragility, black swans, skin in the game, fat tails
taleb.gaps: 'The Black Swan' and 'Antifragile' (2012) both in bibliography (T1). Strong coverage.

veblen.lens: Institutional Economist — conspicuous consumption, pecuniary emulation, engineers vs price system
veblen.gaps: Veblen's 'Theory of the Leisure Class' now in bibliography (T1). Relevant for AI status signaling analysis.

weber.lens: Sociologist of Rationalization — bureaucracy, iron cage, charismatic vs rational-legal authority
weber.gaps: Weber's 'Economy and Society' now in bibliography (T1). Essential for governance rationalization analysis.

yarvin.lens: Reactionary Engineer — neoreaction, Cathedral critique, patchwork governance
yarvin.gaps: No Yarvin/Moldbug entry (blog-native, no academic publication). Represent via Burnham (managerial revolution thesis) and Pareto (elite circulation).

zuboff.lens: Surveillance Capitalism Analyst — behavioral modification, prediction imperative, instrumentarian power
zuboff.gaps: Zuboff's book is in bibliography. Strong coverage for this lens.

synthesist.lens: ARCANA Coordinator — cross-domain synthesis, convergence mapping
synthesist.gaps: No direct synthesis methodology entry beyond Pearl's causal calculus.
